title: Strict Partial Order
statement:
A relation $<$ on a set $S$ is a \emph{strict partial order} if it satisfies,
for all $a, b, c \in S$:
- \textbf{Irreflexivity:} $\neg(a < a)$.
- \textbf{Asymmetry:} $a < b \Longrightarrow \neg(b < a)$.
- \textbf{Transitivity:} $a < b \land b < c \Longrightarrow a < c$.
